question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger deletion of TopicMessage records by submitting a crafted ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a manipulated consensusTimestamp that causes the prune() boundary condition to delete messages they do not own, leading to unauthorized data destruction of other users' topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of submitKey validation in the TopicMessageRepository persistence path to cause a topic message to be stored as if it were authorized by the topic's submitKey, when in fact no valid signature was provided, leading to unauthorized message injection into a submit-key-protected topic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction targeting a topic with a non-null submitKey, and have the mirror node persist it via CrudRepository.save() without verifying the submitKey signature, causing the mirror node's topic_message table to contain messages that were never authorized by the topic owner?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high-volume of ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions with timestamps just below the retention boundary to manipulate the prune() cutoff, causing legitimate authorized messages from topic owners to be pruned while attacker-injected messages survive, leading to a falsified topic message history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prune() uses a JPQL query 'delete from TopicMessage where consensusTimestamp <= ?1' with no ownership or authorization check, to cause the scheduled RetentionJob to delete topic messages belonging to any topic regardless of adminKey or submitKey, leading to unauthorized destruction of protected topic history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage to a topic that has a submitKey set, and because the mirror node's TopicMessageRepository.save() does not re-validate the submitKey at persistence time, have the message stored as an authorized message, allowing the attacker to impersonate an authorized submitter in the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isNew() always-returning-true pattern in TopicMessage to bypass any update-path authorization checks, causing the repository to always perform an INSERT without checking whether the payerAccountId is authorized to submit to the target topicId, leading to unauthorized message persistence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file containing a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a spoofed payerAccountId field, and because TopicMessageRepository.save() stores the payerAccountId directly from the transaction body without verifying it matches the actual transaction signer, cause the mirror node to attribute the message to a different account, leading to false attribution of authorized transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a crafted initialTransactionId in the chunkInfo that references a different account's TransactionID, and because TopicMessageRepository.save() stores initialTransactionId as raw bytes without verifying the referenced account signed the initial chunk, cause the mirror node to falsely associate the message with an authorized account's transaction chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with chunkNum and chunkTotal values that do not correspond to any previously authorized initial transaction, and because TopicMessageRepository.save() stores these chunk fields without verifying the chunk sequence was initiated by an authorized signer, cause the mirror node to persist a fraudulent multi-chunk message as if it were authorized?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of per-topic authorization in prune() to cause the deletion of topic messages from a topic whose adminKey they do not hold, by influencing the consensusTimestamp parameter through timing attacks on the RetentionJob scheduler, leading to unauthorized erasure of a topic's message history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage to a topic with no submitKey set (open topic), and because TopicMessageRepository.save() does not distinguish between open and submit-key-protected topics at the persistence layer, cause the mirror node to store the message in a way that is indistinguishable from an authorized submission to a protected topic, leading to confusion in downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of runningHash verification in TopicMessageRepository.save() to submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a forged runningHash value, causing the mirror node to store an incorrect running hash that breaks the cryptographic chain of custody for the topic, allowing the attacker to make the topic's message history appear tampered?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a manipulated sequenceNumber that does not follow the expected monotonically increasing sequence, and because TopicMessageRepository.save() does not validate sequenceNumber continuity or authorization, cause the mirror node to store a message with a fraudulent sequence number, breaking the integrity guarantees of the topic's ordered message log?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicMessageRepository extends CrudRepository which exposes deleteAll() and delete() methods without any authorization check, to delete topic messages from the database without holding the topic's adminKey or submitKey, leading to unauthorized destruction of topic message history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prune() deletes all TopicMessage records with consensusTimestamp <= ?1 without checking the topicId or any associated submitKey/adminKey, to cause cross-topic message deletion where messages from authorized topics are removed alongside unauthorized ones, leading to collateral destruction of protected topic data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a validStartTimestamp that predates the topic's creation timestamp, and because TopicMessageRepository.save() does not validate that validStartTimestamp is within the topic's valid lifetime, cause the mirror node to store a message that appears to have been submitted before the topic existed, undermining the integrity of the topic's authorized message timeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of feeExemptKeyList validation in TopicMessageRepository.save() to submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage claiming fee exemption without holding any key in the feeExemptKeyList, causing the mirror node to persist the message as if the submitter were fee-exempt, leading to unauthorized bypass of custom fee authorization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage to a topic whose submitKey has been updated via ConsensusUpdateTopic, and because TopicMessageRepository.save() does not check the current submitKey at the time of persistence, cause the mirror node to store a message that was signed with an old/revoked submitKey as if it were currently authorized?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Modifying annotation on prune() combined with the @Transactional annotation on RetentionRepository to cause a race condition where a legitimate authorized message is deleted mid-transaction before it can be read by an authorized subscriber, leading to unauthorized denial of access to topic message data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a message payload that contains crafted binary data designed to exploit the EntityIdConverter used for payerAccountId and topicId fields, causing the repository to store a message with a different topicId than intended, leading to unauthorized message injection into a different topic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a chunkInfo.initialTransactionID that references a transaction from a different account than the actual payer, and because TopicMessageRepository.save() stores the initialTransactionId bytes without verifying the referenced account authorized the chunk sequence, cause the mirror node to falsely link the message to an authorized account's transaction?
